description: Shared Spanish collection schema types for reusable sentence and word content models.

sentenceChunk.description: A learner-facing chunk inside a Spanish sentence.

fields.label: Chunk Spanish
fields.description: Chunk text exactly as it appears in the sentence.

fields.label: English
fields.description: Short learner-friendly English rendering of the chunk.

fields.label: References
fields.description: Referenced terms from the Spanish words collection.

sentenceEntry.description: Spanish learner sentence with translation and optional learner chunks.

fields.label: Spanish
fields.description: Full Spanish sentence in natural surface form.

fields.label: English
fields.description: Natural English translation of the full sentence.

fields.label: Chunks
fields.description: Ordered learner-friendly chunks that reference terms from the Spanish words collection.

wordEntry.description: Spanish vocabulary entry as a learner-useful word or expression.

fields.label: Term
fields.description: Main Spanish study term. Usually a canonical single word, but it may also be a short useful phrase or collocation when that is the most learnable unit.

fields.label: Meaning
fields.description: Short English gloss. Keep it compact; put nuance in notes later if needed.

values.proper-noun: Name of a person, place, organization, etc.

fields.label: Semantic Class
fields.description: Semantic category/class of the term. Use plain text rather than an enum so this can scale across parts of speech; examples include home, food, animals, people, city, and time. Use null when not needed.

fields.label: Gender
fields.description: Gender classification for nouns; use null when not applicable.

values.mf: Common gender / masculine and feminine forms.
values.f_el: Feminine noun that takes singular `el` before stressed /a/ (for example, `el agua`, `el águila`).
